chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Constitution Petition No. D- 1430 of 2021 ( Shah Nawaz Waseer vs. P.O Sindh and others) DATE OF HEARING ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE Present; Irshad Ali Shah, J Zulfiqar Ali Sangi, J 13-03-2024 Petitioner Shah Nawaz Waseer in person Mr. Shahryar Awan, Assistant Advocate General Sindh >>>>>>..<<<<<< Irshad Ali Shah, J;- The facts in brief necessary for disposal of instant petition are that the petitioner was appointed as a Legal Advisor to render legal services to Municipal Committee, Ghotki with fixed monthly pay of Rs.30000/-; his services were terminated upon dissatisfaction, such termination the petitioner has impugned before this Court by way of instant Constitutional Petition. 2. It is contended by the petitioner that his services have been terminated without lawful justification or providing a chance of hearing to him, therefore, such action being illegal is liable to be set aside by this court, which is opposed by learned AAG Sindh by contending that the petitioner has got no vested right to insist upon to serve the Municipal Committee, Ghotki as its legal advisor when it has lost its confidence over him. 3. Heard arguments, perused record. 2 4. The services of the petitioner were hired with fixed pay only to render legal assistance to the Municipal Committee, Ghotki, which as per comments filed on behalf of Municipal Committee, Ghotki the petitioner failed to render satisfactorily which resulted in his termination. The relationship between the petitioner and the Municipal Committee, Ghotki was that of lawyer and client. If the services of lawyer are discontinued by his client on dissatisfaction then he could not ask to continue with the same legally.

The relationship between the petitioner and the Municipal Committee, Ghotki was that of lawyer and client. If the services of lawyer are discontinued by his client on dissatisfaction then he could not ask to continue with the same legally. No hateful relationship could be forced to continue. In that situation, it would be unjustified to declare the termination order of the petitioner illegal and unlawful. 5. In view of above, instant petition fails and it is dismissed accordingly. Judge Judge ARBROHI
